question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user lose staking rewards when the RecordFileIterator processes a database with only one record file (the comment at line 108-109 notes this edge case), causing the iterator to prune that single record file's entire timestamp range and delete all staking_reward_transfer records including those for pending rewards not yet settled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's pending staking rewards be corrupted when the prune() job deletes network_stake records via NetworkStakeRepository.prune() with the guard epoch_day < max(epoch_day) - 366, and the deleted records include the max_staking_reward_rate_per_hbar value used to cap pending reward calculations, causing the mirror node to report an uncapped (inflated) pending reward that is later corrected to zero?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user be harmed when the prune() job's transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() commits each batch atomically, but the staking_reward_transfer prune and the node_stake prune within the same batch use different effective cutoff timestamps due to the forEach ordering of retentionRepositories, creating a window where staking_reward_transfer records are deleted but the corresponding node_stake reward rates are still present, then deleted in the next batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking rewards be stolen when the prune() job is triggered with a very short batchPeriod (e.g., 1 nanosecond), causing the RecordFileIterator to iterate through every single record file and call StakingRewardTransferRepository.prune() with incrementally increasing timestamps, effectively deleting all staking_reward_transfer records one batch at a time before entity_stake is updated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user lose pending staking rewards when the RecordFileIterator.hasNext() calls recordFileRepository.findNextBetween(endTimestamp, max.getConsensusEnd()) and the query returns a record file whose consensusEnd is exactly equal to a staking period boundary, causing the prune to delete staking_reward_transfer records for that period before entity_stake.end_stake_period is advanced past it?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking reward transfer records be deleted when the shouldPrune() check in prune(iterator) evaluates to true for the staking_reward_transfer table because neither the include nor exclude sets contain it (both default to empty), meaning there is no explicit protection preventing the retention job from pruning staking reward data regardless of whether pending rewards have been settled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user be harmed when the prune() job deletes staking_reward_transfer records for account 0.0.800 (the staking reward account), which is used as a sentinel in the RecalculatePendingRewardMigration SQL to determine end_stake_period, causing the migration to compute incorrect pending rewards for all staking accounts including the attacker's victim?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking rewards be affected when the prune() job deletes node_stake records that are needed by the EntityStakeRepository.updated() query (which checks if epoch_day from node_stake matches end_stake_period from entity_stake), causing EntityStakeCalculatorImpl to incorrectly believe entity_stake is up-to-date and skip the pending reward recalculation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user lose staking rewards when the RecordFileIterator processes batches and the endTimestamp used in StakingRewardTransferRepository.prune(endTimestamp) corresponds to a consensus timestamp that falls in the middle of a staking period, causing a partial deletion of staking_reward_transfer records for that period and making the pending_reward calculation for that period incorrect?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's pending staking rewards be permanently lost when the prune() job runs with a retention period shorter than the time elapsed since the user's account was created, deleting all staking_reward_transfer records for that account and making it impossible for the mirror node to reconstruct the correct pending_reward value even if entity_stake is later recalculated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user be harmed when the prune() job's RecordFileIterator initializes current to the earliest record file via findNextBetween(0, max.getConsensusEnd()), and this earliest record file's consensusEnd predates the first staking reward payout, causing the first prune batch to use a timestamp that deletes zero staking_reward_transfer records but still advances the iterator, potentially skipping a batch that should have been protected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking rewards be stolen when the prune() job deletes staking_reward_transfer records and the mirror node's REST API subsequently returns a pending_reward of 0 for the user's account (because entity_stake.pending_reward was never updated to reflect the deleted transfers), causing the user to believe they have no pending rewards and not claim them on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user lose staking rewards when the RecordFileIterator.hasNext() computes endTimestamp = current.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod.toNanos() and batchPeriod.toNanos() returns Long.MAX_VALUE due to a very large Duration configuration, causing integer overflow to a negative endTimestamp that is passed to StakingRewardTransferRepository.prune(), deleting all records with consensusTimestamp <= negative_value (i.e., none) or all records depending on database behavior?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking reward data be corrupted when the prune() job runs and deletes node_stake records via NodeStakeRepository.prune() with the condition epoch_day < max(epoch_day) - 366, but the max(epoch_day) subquery is evaluated at the time of the DELETE statement rather than at the start of the transaction, allowing a concurrent node_stake insert to shift max(epoch_day) and cause more records to be deleted than intended?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user be harmed when the prune() job deletes staking_reward_transfer records for a staking period where the user's stake_period_start equals epoch_day - 1, and the deleted records were the basis for the first_period_stake_change CTE in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration, causing the migration to compute an incorrect stake_total_start and thus an incorrect pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking rewards be stolen when the prune() job deletes staking_reward_transfer records that are the only evidence of a reward payout from account 0.0.800, and the entity_stake.pending_reward for the user's account was computed using those records as input, causing any subsequent recalculation to produce a lower pending_reward value?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user lose pending staking rewards when the RecordFileIterator processes the max record file as the final batch (current = max at line 131), and the endTimestamp used is max.getConsensusEnd() which may be within the current active staking period, causing StakingRewardTransferRepository.prune() to delete staking_reward_transfer records for the current period before entity_stake is updated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking rewards be affected when the prune() job's synchronized keyword only prevents concurrent execution within the same JVM instance, and a multi-instance deployment allows two importer instances to run prune() simultaneously, causing double-deletion of staking_reward_transfer records and race conditions in entity_stake updates?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged user be harmed when the prune() job deletes network_stake records via NetworkStakeRepository.prune() that contain the staking_reward_fee_fraction used to compute the net reward after fees, causing the mirror node to report an incorrect (pre-fee) pending_reward value that overstates the user's actual claimable reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user's staking reward transfer records be deleted when the prune() job runs with a retention period of exactly 90 days (the default), and the user's last staking_reward_transfer occurred exactly 90 days ago, causing the record to be deleted by the <= boundary condition in StakingRewardTransferRepository.prune() even though the user has not yet claimed the reward on-chain?
